title: Madronella macrantha
intro: Madronella macrantha, also known as Mountain Mahogany, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and dense, thorny branches. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Madronella macrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Madronella macrantha is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
